mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real personal consumption expenditures (proxied by RSAFS) signals that consumer demand is expanding, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months when RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 5.0%, the cost of capital increases sharply, compressing equity valuations via higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, the hypothetical negative signal fails.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low unemployment and high payroll gains indicate a tight labor market that pushes corporate margins via wage costs, but also signals robust aggregate demand; the net effect is positive for equities over quarterly horizons as earnings growth outpaces cost pressures.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of months when both conditions hold, the joint signal is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production after a recession signals that supply chains are healing and manufacturing output is recovering, which boosts confidence in the broader economy and supports positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If in NBER-dated recovery periods, 20-day forward returns are negative more than 40% of the time despite rising INDPRO, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate within a single month signals sudden labor market weakness, which triggers immediate risk-off sentiment and negative short-term equity returns within five trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.2pp in one month
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns are positive after at least 10 observed UNRATE spikes (0.2pp+), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is positive and housing starts exceed 1.5 million units, the economy is expanding rapidly on both aggregate demand and residential investment fronts, which lifts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters when both conditions hold, the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation (CPI above 3% year-over-year) forces the central bank to maintain or tighten monetary policy, which depresses equity valuations through higher real discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over quarterly horizons.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPI Y/Y exceeds 3%, the negative signal is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production indicate a strengthening manufacturing sector, which supports near-term equity prices as investors price in higher production and economic momentum over the coming week.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5-day S&P 500 returns is non-positive over any rolling 1-year window, hypothesis is rejected.
